selections.name: HP SOPHIA
selections.reason: Draws the Mohawk sweet spot (Post 6) and showed plenty of trot late in her sophomore debut after being locked in. Top pick to kick off the card.

selections.name: CLASSIFIED
selections.reason: Made a nice recovery two starts back and worked out a great trip last week to challenge the favorite. Post 4 is a major advantage here.

selections.name: NOTATTHETABLCARLOS
selections.reason: Dropping from OSS Grassroots company. Post 1 can be a trap at Mohawk, but he has the class to overcome it if he avoids getting shuffled.

selections.name: STORMONT FRIEDBOY
selections.reason: Improving sort who finished only seven lengths back last time. Picks up a top driver and could offer value underneath.

danger_horse.name: STORMONT SHANLYBOY
danger_horse.reason: Consistent closer who can hit the ticket if the pace collapses, despite the wide draw.

selections.name: L P BAY
selections.reason: Draws the sweet spot (Post 4). Raced aggressively in his SBOA elimination and got stuck in traffic last week. Ready for a breakout.

selections.name: MAXIMUM EXPOSURE
selections.reason: Bet down to 2-1 last week and made the front before breaking stride. If he stays flat from Post 5, he is a massive threat.

selections.name: HERCULES
selections.reason: Faced incredibly tough company in April/May. Gets massive post relief today after drawing Post 10 last week.

selections.name: ELBOWS UP
selections.reason: Dominant OSS Grassroots winner last time out. Faces males tonight from the rail, which limits her ceiling, but she is too sharp to ignore.

danger_horse.name: VALIDUS
danger_horse.reason: Consistent performer drawing a prime post position; can easily grab a share.

selections.name: CHOW FOR NOW
selections.reason: Massive class drop from OSS Grassroots to NW1. Draws the perfect Post 5 and should be handled aggressively against this weaker field.

selections.name: KEY LARGO HANOVER
selections.reason: Impressive debut winner at Flamboro. Faces the 'bullring tax' shipping to the 7/8 mile oval, but has the gate speed to protect position.

selections.name: WICKED WANKS
selections.reason: Another class dropper from the OSS ranks. Post 6 is ideal for a mid-race move.

selections.name: CABO SAN LUCAS
selections.reason: Paced a 27.2 final quarter in his sophomore debut here. Strong closer who can pick up the pieces late.

danger_horse.name: CORONA SEELSTER
danger_horse.reason: Gets a major driver upgrade to Louis-Philippe Roy; could wake up at a price.

selections.name: D A MASSTERMIND
selections.reason: Taking a massive class plunge from a $140,000 OSS Gold event down to NW2. Draws Post 5. Absolute standout on paper.

selections.name: LAND THE PLANE
selections.reason: Classy colt who was competitive in Grand Circuit races last year. Strong second in his sophomore debut; main danger despite the wide draw.

selections.name: BITOFTHEBUBBLY
selections.reason: Faced an impossible first-over trip into a 27.4 final quarter last time. Capable of a big bounce-back effort.

selections.name: GREEN NINETEEN
selections.reason: Dropping out of OSS Grassroots company. Draws well and has tactical early speed to secure a trip.

danger_horse.name: MAIZE AND BLUE
danger_horse.reason: Solid third in OSS Grassroots action last time; fits well with this group.

selections.name: FIGURATIVELY
selections.reason: Led deep into the stretch in her sophomore debut before getting caught by the favorite. Draws the best post tonight and is primed to graduate.

selections.name: BEAU KNIGHT
selections.reason: Flashed big speed from Post 9 in previous starts. The rail shouldn't be an issue, but he tends to flatten out late.

selections.name: FEEL LIKE A WOMAN
selections.reason: Raced well in the Ontario Spring Series. Hung out wide last week but still grabbed fourth. Upset potential.

selections.name: ERINWOOD PEACE OUT
selections.reason: Well-bred colt who showed late zip in his debut. Draws perfectly here to take a step forward.

danger_horse.name: LUNA C
danger_horse.reason: Inside draw and dropping from OSS Grassroots; can sit a pocket trip and threaten.

selections.name: HP CRYSTALINE
selections.reason: Comes off a lifetime-best victory in an OSS Gold leg. Draws the sweet spot (Post 6) and holds a massive class advantage.

selections.name: SHIMMERING HANOVER
selections.reason: Grand Circuit winner at two. Needed her seasonal debut in PASS company and should be much sharper tonight from a great post.

selections.name: WALNERS GROVE
selections.reason: Showed gameness to break her maiden last week. Wide draw hurts, but she is trending in the right direction.

selections.name: BELLA CHICA
selections.reason: Solid second in OSS Grassroots action. Post 9 is tough, but she has the tactical speed to overcome it.

danger_horse.name: KAYLAS INCHARGE
danger_horse.reason: Consistent winner shipping in from London. Suffers the bullring tax but knows how to find the wire.

selections.name: CAPTAIN WILLIE
selections.reason: Has been knocking on the door with strong efforts against tough favorites. Looks best in this field and is ready to break his maiden.

selections.name: DULCES SUENOS DEO
selections.reason: Reverted to closing tactics last week and grabbed third. Draws Post 6 and will be flying late if the pace is hot.

selections.name: TOTALLY ICONIC
selections.reason: Broke his maiden impressively at Flamboro. Faces the 7/8 mile stretch test tonight but draws perfectly to get a trip.

selections.name: HAVEACANFORME
selections.reason: Qualified well over this track after breaking stride at Flamboro. Capable if she behaves.

danger_horse.name: FEET OF THE MOON
danger_horse.reason: Draws well and has shown enough late speed to hit the bottom of the exotics.

selections.name: SOUTHWIND FLORENCE
selections.reason: Terrific in her last two starts, including a 1:54 flat lifetime best. Confident front-end winner who draws perfectly to threepeat.

selections.name: CALYX
selections.reason: Closed well from third-over last week. Has been victimized by bad posts/trips recently; the rail gives her a chance to turn the tables.

selections.name: CUPID HALL
selections.reason: Classy filly who took third in an OSS Gold event last time out. Wide draw is the only negative.

selections.name: KNGTSTLKRS GAMES
selections.reason: Talented filly returning from a Kentucky campaign. Qualified well and picks up Louis-Philippe Roy.

danger_horse.name: KENOGAMI FANCY
danger_horse.reason: Broke stride at Flamboro but was a sharp winner here prior. Draws the sweet spot and is dangerous if flat.

selections.name: USAIN HANOVER
selections.reason: Has unleashed monstrous moves to win the Open in his last two starts. The wide draw won't stop him from completing the threepeat.

selections.name: LOGAN PARK
selections.reason: Champion trotter returning home after a failed Cutler bid. Always a threat to wire the field, but must hold off Usain Hanover late.

selections.name: HASTY BID
selections.reason: Incredibly consistent Open performer. Draws the sweet spot and will likely secure a pocket trip behind the speed.

selections.name: OSCEOLA
selections.reason: Classy veteran who tripped out to win last week. Will be sent forward for position and can stick around for a share.

danger_horse.name: SID THE KID
danger_horse.reason: Steps up after a sharp win; rail draw could allow him to save ground and sneak into the Superfecta.

selections.name: COASTLINE
selections.reason: Produced a massive 1:51.2 lifetime best last week with a 27.2 final quarter. Draws perfectly and is the horse to beat if he replicates that effort.

selections.name: NATIONAL TIER
selections.reason: Drops back down to the $20k claiming level where he was a dominant winner two starts back. Expect an aggressive steer.

selections.name: CHEF
selections.reason: Consistent claimer who will likely get a following trip and stick around for a slice.

selections.name: RYLENTLESS
selections.reason: Drops out of a tougher handicap claimer. Draws Post 6 and should be positioned well early to hit the ticket.

danger_horse.name: BOOTHILL HUDY
danger_horse.reason: Making a quick return after a decent closing effort at Flamboro; could offer massive value underneath.
